articles.title: Peri-implantitis-Is it mainly a clinician-initiated complication of implant therapy?
articles.summary: This review identifies surgical and prosthetic errors—particularly micro-rough implant surface exposure—as the primary cause of peri-implantitis rather than patient pathology, emphasizing prevention through proper surgical positioning and bone regeneration. Implant clinicians can immediately apply these principles to reduce peri-implantitis incidence by adhering to evidence-based surgical protocols.

articles.title: Long-term outcomes of regenerative periodontal therapy and orthodontic treatment: 20-year results from a prospective study.
articles.summary: This 20-year prospective study demonstrates that combining periodontal regenerative surgery (enamel matrix derivative with or without bone graft) and orthodontic treatment can successfully save severely compromised periodontally diseased teeth with probing depths ≥7 mm. The long-term data supports this integrated multidisciplinary approach as a viable alternative to extraction for carefully selected Stage IV periodontitis patients.

articles.title: Regenerative periodontal surgery and orthodontic therapy in the treatment of patients with stage IV periodontitis and pathologic tooth migration.
articles.summary: This narrative review outlines an interdisciplinary approach combining regenerative periodontal surgery with orthodontics for stage IV periodontitis patients with pathologic tooth migration. The evidence-based sequencing of treatments provides practical guidance for specialists managing complex periodontal cases, though implementation requires careful case selection and coordination.

articles.title: Implant therapy in older patients: Prosthetic considerations and biomechanical implications for frailty/aging.
articles.summary: This narrative review synthesizes evidence on implant therapy in older adults, addressing frailty, comorbidities, and biomechanical design modifications (e.g., shortened dental arches, implant-assisted removable prostheses) tailored to aging patients. The evidence-based guidance helps practitioners optimize implant treatment planning and prosthetic design for this growing demographic.

articles.title: Periodontitis, Edentulism, and Risk of Metabolic Syndrome, Obesity, and Dyslipidemia: A Systematic Review With Meta-Analyses.
articles.summary: A meta-analysis of 10 longitudinal studies confirms that periodontitis and edentulism significantly increase the risk of metabolic syndrome, obesity, and dyslipidemia, reinforcing the systemic health implications of poor oral health. General dentists can use these findings to counsel patients that treating periodontal disease may reduce cardiometabolic risk.

articles.title: From force to resorption: mechanotransduction, osteoclasts, prevention.
articles.summary: This comprehensive review clarifies how orthodontic forces trigger a cascade of cellular mechanisms that drive root resorption through osteoclast activation, and identifies modifiable mechanical variables (force magnitude, direction, duration) that clinicians can control to reduce this risk. Practitioners can now apply specific force parameters and monitoring strategies based on mechanistic understanding rather than empirical rules alone.

articles.title: Effectiveness of silver diamine fluoride and fluoride varnish in arresting caries lesions in permanent molars: A randomized controlled trial.
articles.summary: Silver diamine fluoride (38%) arrested caries in 88% of permanent molars versus 66% with fluoride varnish in a randomized controlled trial of 165 children. This finding provides clear clinical guidance for pediatric dentists choosing chemically arrestive agents, though SDF's cosmetic staining remains a limiting factor.

articles.title: Long-term outcomes of ceramic crowns in adolescents and young adults with amelogenesis imperfecta: final follow-up of a randomized split-mouth controlled clinical trial.
articles.summary: This long-term (13.4-year) randomized controlled trial in adolescents and young adults with amelogenesis imperfecta found that both zirconia and pressed-ceramic crowns performed well clinically over a decade, providing strong evidence for early prosthetic treatment in this population. General dentists and specialists can confidently recommend ceramic crowns as a durable solution for AI patients, though the findings are specific to this rare enamel disorder.

articles.title: Reverse compensation technique achieves accurate robotic-assisted implant surgery in the anterior maxilla: A prospective case series.
articles.summary: This prospective case series demonstrates that the Reverse Compensation Technique improves robotic-assisted implant placement accuracy in the anterior maxilla with challenging bone anatomy, with acceptable surgical outcomes and low adverse events. The technique offers a practical innovation for specialists managing complex anterior cases, though further multicenter validation would strengthen the evidence base.

articles.title: The Survival and Success of Teeth With External Cervical Resorption: A Multi-Centre, Retrospective Study.
articles.summary: This multi-centre retrospective study of 296 teeth with external cervical resorption demonstrates that survival and success outcomes vary substantially based on treatment approach (periodic review, external repair, or internal repair). Treatment selection informed by lesion characteristics and imaging can guide endodontists toward the most predictable management strategy.
